subprocess.context: Often used legitimately for system automation

importlib.reason: Allows dynamic module loading — can load malicious code at runtime.
importlib.context: Common in plugin systems and dynamic loading

ctypes.reason: Direct access to C libraries — can call arbitrary native code.
ctypes.context: Often used for legitimate system integration

cffi.reason: C Foreign Function Interface — similar risk to ctypes.

pty.reason: Provides pseudo-terminal — can spawn interactive shells.
pty.context: Used in SSH and terminal applications

os.reason: Provides access to OS-level operations (files, processes, env).
os.context: Virtually all Python scripts use this

sys.reason: Can modify Python runtime, path, and exit behavior.
sys.context: Almost every Python script uses sys

requests.reason: HTTP client — can send data to external servers.
requests.context: Most common HTTP library - used in almost every web project

urllib.reason: Another HTTP client — can fetch or post data externally.

ftplib.reason: FTP client — can upload files to remote servers.
ftplib.context: Legitimate FTP client for file transfers

smtplib.reason: SMTP client — can send emails (data exfiltration vector).

paramiko.reason: SSH client — can connect to remote machines and execute commands.
paramiko.context: Legitimate SSH client for remote access

base64.reason: Commonly used to encode/obfuscate payloads or exfiltrate data.
base64.context: Almost every Python project uses base64 for legitimate encoding

zlib.reason: Compression — can be used to pack/unpack obfuscated payloads.

pickle.reason: Deserializing untrusted pickle data allows arbitrary code execution.
pickle.context: Common in data serialization but dangerous with untrusted data

marshal.reason: Similar to pickle — can execute code on deserialization.
marshal.context: Python's internal serialization - rarely used directly

shelve.reason: Built on pickle — same deserialization risk.

shutil.reason: File operations including copy, move, delete of entire trees.
shutil.context: Standard file operation library - used everywhere

tempfile.reason: Creates temporary files — sometimes used to stage payloads.
tempfile.context: Standard library for temporary files - very common

glob.reason: File discovery — can be used to map out filesystem contents.
glob.context: Standard library for file pattern matching - very common

fnmatch.reason: File pattern matching — similar to glob.
fnmatch.context: Standard library for filename matching - very common

winreg.reason: Windows Registry access — classic persistence mechanism.
winreg.context: Windows-only - used in legitimate Windows applications

sched.reason: Task scheduler — can be used to create time-triggered logic bombs.
sched.context: Used for scheduling tasks in applications

threading.reason: Multi-threading — can be used to hide background activity.
threading.context: Virtually every Python application uses threading

multiprocessing.reason: Multi-process spawning — can obscure malicious child processes.
multiprocessing.context: Standard library for parallel processing - very common

signal.reason: Signal handling — can intercept and suppress shutdown signals.
signal.context: Used for graceful shutdown and signal handling

platform.context: Standard library - used everywhere for cross-platform code

psutil.reason: Process/system monitoring — can enumerate running processes and connections.
psutil.context: Commonly used for system monitoring tools

grp.reason: Reads Unix group database — system enumeration.

getpass.reason: Reads passwords from the terminal — strong exfiltration signal.
getpass.context: Used for secure password input in scripts

keyring.context: Used for credential management in legitimate apps

cryptography.reason: Legitimate use is common, but also used to encrypt/decrypt payloads.
cryptography.context: Most common crypto library - used everywhere for security

Crypto.context: Legacy crypto library - used in many projects

hashlib.reason: Hashing library — low risk but sometimes used to hash stolen data.
hashlib.context: Standard library - used everywhere for hashing

json.context: Virtually every Python project uses json

re.context: Virtually every Python project uses re

math.context: Virtually every Python project uses math

datetime.context: Virtually every Python project uses datetime

time.context: Virtually every Python project uses time
